ground_truth.title: New Books | Columbia University Press
ground_truth.main_content: Recently released titles from Columbia University Press. Cultural Mavericks: The Business and Politics of Independent Bookselling in China by Zheng Liu. The Confucius Chronicles: A Guide to the Texts That Made the Legend translated and edited by Wai-yee Li. Rhetorical Powers: How Rising States Shape International Order by Sasikumar Sundaram. China's Weaponization of Trade: Resistance Through Collective Resilience by Victor D. Cha, Ellen Kim, and Andy Lim. Forest Imaginaries: How African Novels Think by Ainehi Edoro. The Politics of Names: Attitudes, Identity, and the Naming of Children in American History by R. Urbatsch. LGBTQ+ Runaway and Homeless Youth: A Guide to Practice, Policy, and Research by Elaine M. Maccio and Kristin Ferguson. The Souths in Her: Black Women Writers and Choreographers and the Poetics of Transmutation by Nicole M. Morris Johnson. Spinoza for Our Time: Politics and Postmodernity by Antonio Negri. Enduring Erasures: Afterlives of the Armenian Genocide by Hakem Amer Al-Rustom. Possessions: A Novel by Julia Kristeva. Branding as a Cultural Force: Purpose, Responsibility, and Resonance by Robin Landa. The Jervis Effect: The Scholarship and Legacy of Robert Jervis edited by Richard H. Immerman, Stacie E. Goddard, and Diane N. Labrosse. Beauty and the Nation: Women, Culture, and the National Image in Interwar Vietnam by Christina E. Firpo. The Oldest Rocks on Earth: A Search for the Origins of Our World by Simon Lamb. Progress from the Margins: Human Rights and Disability Internationalism Since the 1960s by Paul van Trigt.
with: Cultural Mavericks: The Business and Politics of Independent Bookselling in China
